data.narrative:
Firefly Aerospace, a public-stage space and aerospace company founded by Tom Markusic, currently trades at a $6.3B valuation following an $868M IPO. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of three same-sector, same-stage peers: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Astroscale ($900M).

Peer-median analysis represents the applicable triangulation method, given Firefly's public status and sector alignment. The three-company peer set yields a median valuation of $7.0B, with a range spanning $900M to $37.0B. This wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in business model and scale within the space and aerospace sector. A second method—public-comp anchoring using six sector tickers with a median price-to-sales multiple of 42.9x—cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not on file.

The analysis triangulates to a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B (median $7.0B), anchored primarily on peer-median methodology. Firefly's current $6.3B valuation sits at the low end of this range, approximately 10% below the peer median. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to Rocket Lab and Planet Labs, margin profile across launch and space systems segments, and broader public market repricing of space infrastructure assets. The wide range of comparable valuations ($900M to $37.0B) underscores sensitivity to growth, profitability, and addressable market assumptions.
